steps.title: Identify the y-intercept from the given point
steps.content: The linear function passes through the point $(0, 7)$. Since this point has $x = 0$, it lies on the y-axis, making it the y-intercept. Therefore, $b = 7$.

steps.title: Apply slope-intercept form
steps.content:
The slope-intercept form of a linear function is $y = mx + b$, where $m$ is the slope and $b$ is the y-intercept. Substituting $m = \frac{1}{3}$ and $b = 7$:

$$f(x) = \frac{1}{3}x + 7$$

That matches choice A — A is correct.
